name: Sunjata
description: Sunjata Keita, also known as Sundiata, is a pivotal figure in the oral traditions of the Mandinka people of West Africa and the founder of the Mali Empire. Born with a prophecy of greatness, his early life was marked by physical disability and exile. Despite these challenges, Sunjata possessed inherent magical abilities and an indomitable spirit. He is revered as a warrior-king, a powerful sorcerer, and a wise lawgiver. His epic, primarily transmitted through griots (oral historians), recounts his struggles against the tyrannical king Soumaoro Kanté, his eventual victory at the Battle of Kirina, and the establishment of the Mali Empire, ushering in a golden age of prosperity and cultural flourishing. Sunjata's story is not merely a historical account but a foundational narrative that embodies Mandinka values of courage, justice, and leadership, continuing to inspire and inform cultural identity.
subtitle: Founder of the Mali Empire

keyMyths.title: The Prophecy and Birth of Sunjata
keyMyths.description: The myth begins with a prophecy foretelling the birth of a great king who will unite the Mandinka people. Sunjata's mother, Sogolon Kondé, is chosen by the king Maghan Kon Fatta due to her perceived magical abilities. Sunjata's birth is delayed and difficult, and he is unable to walk until the age of seven, causing him and his mother to be ostracized by the king's other wives and children. This isolation fuels his determination to fulfill his destiny.
keyMyths.source: Niane, Djibril Tamsir. Sundiata: An Epic of Old Mali.

keyMyths.title: Sunjata's Exile and Return
keyMyths.description: Following the death of Maghan Kon Fatta, Sunjata and his mother are forced into exile by the new king, Dankaran Touman. During his exile, Sunjata travels to various kingdoms, honing his skills and gathering allies. His reputation as a skilled warrior and a just leader grows, and he eventually answers the call of the Mandinka people, who are suffering under the rule of the sorcerer-king Soumaoro Kanté. The exile is crucial for Sunjata's development and the forging of his alliances.
keyMyths.source: Innes, Gordon. Sunjata: Three Mandinka Versions.

keyMyths.title: The Battle of Kirina and the Founding of the Mali Empire
keyMyths.description: Sunjata confronts Soumaoro Kanté at the Battle of Kirina. Using his knowledge of Soumaoro's weaknesses and with the aid of his allies, Sunjata defeats the sorcerer-king. This victory marks the end of Soumaoro's tyrannical reign and the beginning of the Mali Empire. Sunjata establishes a new social and political order, based on justice, fairness, and the Manden Charter, considered one of the earliest declarations of human rights.
keyMyths.source: Conrad, David C. Empires of Medieval West Africa: Ghana, Mali, and Songhay.

sources.author: Niane, Djibril Tamsir
sources.work: Sundiata: An Epic of Old Mali
sources.description: A widely recognized and influential retelling of the Sunjata epic, based on oral traditions.
